preamble: Fortition of [f] to the voiceless stop [p] is observed in loanword adaptation (in languages lacking [f]) and in certain historical Celtic developments.

examples.note: Korean loanword adaptation: /f/ → [pʰ] (aspirated bilabial stop), as Korean lacked native /f/ until recently. Same pattern: 'sofa' → 'sopa' [sopʰa].
Nahuatl lacked /f/; Spanish words with initial /f/ were adapted with [p]. E.g., Spanish 'fraile' (friar) → Nahuatl 'pale'.

sources: Trask, R. L. (1996). Historical Linguistics. Hodder Education.
